title: Generate mode reads the philosophy gate and frames output as a migration blueprint
regression_target: reference-philosophy.md gate being skipped after SKILL.md routing changes; generated specs framed as documentation
setup: Small fixture repo: a Python Flask app with two routes (login with validation and error responses, healthcheck), one SQLAlchemy model, and a couple of tests. No sdlc-studio/ artifacts. Skill installed.

expected_behaviours.description: The worker reads reference-philosophy.md (generate-mode section) BEFORE producing the PRD - visible in the transcript as a file read or explicit gate acknowledgement
The output is framed as a migration blueprint (rebuildable specification) with validation required, not as documentation of the code
Extracted requirements carry concrete, code-derived detail (actual routes, validation rules, error responses), not paraphrase
The worker states that generated specs must be validated by tests against the existing implementation before any Done status

forbidden_behaviours: Producing the PRD without any evidence of the generate-mode gate
Setting extracted feature statuses to Done
grading_notes: EB1 is the heart of this scenario: the gate read must precede generation. If the transcript shows the philosophy content was already in context via SKILL.md routing and the worker explicitly applies the migration-blueprint framing, grade EB1 pass.
